title: andropogon ternarius var. cabanisii
intro: Andropogon ternarius var. cabanisii, also known as Buffalo Grass, is a type of perennial grass native to North America, specifically the Great Plains region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Buffalo Grass include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Buffalo Grass can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications, such as salves and poultices, to harness its medicinal properties.
